openspec.slot: openspec/ (specs/ current truth, changes/ in-flight deltas, changes/archive/ history)
openspec.note: Bound upstream (spec §16): the CLI generates the opsx commands, skills, and slot into .opencode/ + openspec/. Not vendored or hand-authored. The installed CLI is authoritative over prose.

propose.description: Open an OpenSpec change (/opsx:propose): proposal, design note, spec delta, tasks. Superpowers brainstorming feeds the design note when the change introduces UI or new architecture (§17 stage 1).

brainstorm_ui.fires_when: change adds a UI surface and the design note lacks a Design alternatives section

brainstorm_architecture.fires_when: change adds a new service/model/integration/data shape and the design note lacks an Architecture alternatives section

design_shotgun.fires_when: change adds a UI surface with no UI-SPEC.md / design contract yet

validate.description: openspec validate --all MUST be green AND the change MUST carry independent multi-AI review BEFORE any code (§17 stage 2). This single stage collapses the former plan-review and spec-review gates.

change_gate.requires: active change with `openspec validate --all` green AND REVIEWS.md carrying >= 2 '## Reviewer:' headings
change_gate.exempts: openspec/** artifact writes (author the change while gated)
change_gate.fail_open: malformed/unparseable stdin only (parse error, never policy)

multi_ai_review.wrapper: bin/reviewer-cli.sh (</dev/null + timeout — codex-exec stdin-hang, PILOT-REPORT friction #3)
multi_ai_review.target: the active OpenSpec change (proposal + design + spec delta), BEFORE code
multi_ai_review.evidence: openspec/changes/<slug>/REVIEWS.md — one '## Reviewer: <vendor>' section per reviewer
multi_ai_review.note: Retargeted 0.x plan-review. Under spec 1.0.0 this is NOT a standalone gate (§17): its obligation is discharged here in stage 2 and enforced by the §18 change_gate. spec-review's structural role is now `openspec validate --all`.

execute.description: Superpowers-execute the change's tasks (§17 stage 3). The change_gate blocks code edits until stage 2 (validate + review) has passed for the active change.

verification.fires_when: always — before any task transitions to complete

code_review.fires_when: always — after tasks, in independent agent context
code_review.note: Retained (§17): `openspec validate` is a spec check, not a code review, and does not discharge it.

security.fires_when: always on a change touching auth, storage, request handling, secrets, or an LLM trust boundary — never conditional-away in a product host

database_security.fires_when: change touches schema, RLS, definer functions, or storage policy (supabase|postgres|mongodb|firebase|mysql)

db_pre_launch_audit.fires_when: before first production launch, after a major DB migration

qa.fires_when: change ships user-visible behavior AND a dev server is reachable on a local port

ui_preview.fires_when: task modifies a frontend component, route, or visual surface

design_critique.fires_when: UI change with an existing UI-SPEC.md, OR as the filter step in design_shotgun

impeccable_audit.fires_when: change alters a shipping visual surface
impeccable_audit.note: Kept behind the ADR-0021 measured trial (MEASUREMENT.md) — do not remove yet.

ts_declare_first.fires_when: TS change introduces a new module's public API surface in a TS-primary project
ts_declare_first.note: Demoted from a bespoke per-task gate to a lint gate (§17: ts-declare -> lint). Discipline retained; commit sequence declare(ts):/test(ts):/feat(ts): still recommended.

archive.description: Fold the spec delta into openspec/specs/<capability>/spec.md so the slot states the new truth, then `openspec archive` the change into changes/archive/<date>-<slug>/.
archive.rule: archive != ship (§17): `openspec archive` folds the delta and moves the change dir but produces NO git commit.

branch_close.fires_when: the change is archived and the feature branch is ready to merge
branch_close.note: The ship step (git commit + changelog). PR body links changes/archive/<date>-<slug>/ and its evidence; SHOULD reference a Linear issue id for traceability (§19 loose coupling — never synchronized/required).
